builders.name: Aaron Levie
builders.bio: ceo @box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI

tweets.text:
The same kinds of productivity gains we've seen in coding with AI agents are heading to the rest of knowledge work. 

This is the jump when you go from having a chatbot to being able to actually have an agent go off and do work for minutes or even hours and come back with a complete work output that you then review.

Here's an example of the new Box Agent filling out an RFP response from an existing knowledge base. This process would normally take hours to fill out, and requires the full attention of the user doing the work. Now, you provide the Box Agent with the RFP questions, and it will go off, make a plan, extract all the relevant questions, read through existing source material to come up with an answer, and then generate a new word document as the final output. All while you're doing something else.

The key to this architecture is that the agent is able to use all of the same tools in the background that a user uses to get work done. The agent can search for documents, read entire files, run scripts and tools in the background, and even be able to write code on the fly to automate tasks it hasn't seen before. And best of all, the Box Agent will (soon) work from the Box MCP and CLI so you can invoke it in any agentic system as a step in a process.

This kind of agent complexity would have been impossible even 6 months ago. Models consistently failed at tracking long running tasks or using the right tools at the right moment for the task. But this is all now possible because of models like GPT-5.4, Opus 4.6, and Gemini 3, and is only getting better by the month.

Just as we moved from engineers writing code and using AI as an assistant to answer questions, in many areas of knowledge work -like legal, finance, consulting, sales, marketing, and more- when we have a problem we'll just kick off the AI agent to just go work on it for us in the background.

tweets.text:
Btw: @hemal built this as a simple skill in a few hours, since Prism is powered by Codex. More to come.

And if you love the idea of building AI-native products to accelerate science, we're hiring a lead designer for Prism: apply here! https://t.co/Tt9MgzHxnO https://t.co/DgY3q5Tm5L
Try it out at https://t.co/4MBs45PNiU today and let me know what you think!

Paper Review is built to act more like a careful technical reviewer than a grammar checker. It looks for issues in math, derivations, notation, units, structure, and whether a paper’s claims are actually supported by the results. It also catches inconsistencies across sections, figures, tables, captions, and appendices. The goal is to help authors strengthen technical rigor and correctness before submission.

Inside Prism, it runs as a structured workflow and writes its output as an editable LaTeX review file directly in the project. That means the review becomes part of the paper workspace itself, so authors can revise and work through feedback in the same environment as the manuscript. It also flags unclear phrasing, citation issues, and proofreading mistakes before submission.

builders.bio: We're building the crypto analyst you always needed and never got. Not an LLM wrapper. Specialized AI drawing on human-indexed data, cutting hours of research.

tweets.text:
Prediction Markets pulled ~82M+ impressions this week, with attention heavily concentrated at the top.

@Polymarket  and @Kalshi alone captured 82% of total sector mindshare, turning what looks like a broad category into a 2-player narrative battle.

Polymarket, however, dominated with 55.6% mindshare and 60.8M impression here’s why:

→ Exchange upgrade: A full trading engine overhaul (new order book, smart contracts, $pUSD, SDK, builder fees). This activated builders, one of the highest signal-per-author segments.
→ LaLiga partnership: 154M impressions from a single announcement. This pushed prediction markets into mainstream sports audiences.
→ Revenue + AUM reveal: ~$2M/day revenue + $1.25B user funds.
→ $POLY airdrop speculation: Referral programs + farming guides brought in non-native users.

Kalshi also captured 26.5% mindshare, driven almost entirely by regulation.

→ A major legal win (federal law overriding state gambling bans) 
→ A conflicting ruling in Nevada 
→ Continued exposure via macro predictions (e.g., oil markets)

However, beyond the top 2, the market fragments fast (everything else <3.5%).

Where is crypto attention flowing right now? Layer 1 dominates, but AI is surging at 11.2% mindshare, and @bittensor is the reason. 

While AI Agents lose ground (-1.17), the broader AI narrative is thriving. https://t.co/VRZ2WDedEA

tweets.text: canton network burns 15m CC tokens daily from $9t in monthly settlement volume. DTCC alone processes $5.9t/month in tokenized treasury repo through it. burn/mint ratio went from 0.15 to 0.65 in six months. $916m in annual burns on a sub-$6b market cap. that's 14% of market cap destroyed annually from production settlement, not speculation. circle and visa both running super validators. DTCC launching full tokenization services july 2026. the market is pricing CC like a speculative L1 when goldman sachs and jpmorgan are already settling trillions through it. track daily burn rate acceleration. that's your real-time institutional adoption metric.

magic eden raised ordinals fees on march 8. shut down the marketplace march 9. collected $10m in ordinals fees total then walked away. now 80% of $25m annual revenue comes from solana alone. the pivot is dicey, their onchain gambling product. $15m wagered volume from 100 beta users. if that scales to 1000 users at a 5% house edge instead of 2% marketplace fees, the revenue math completely flips. 30% of core revenue now goes to $ME buybacks distributed to stakers. $69k USDC paid out over 2 months on current marketplace revenue. the bull case for $ME is no longer "biggest NFT marketplace." it's "solana-native gambling yield token." the multi-chain NFT thesis is officially dead. the company that dominated ordinals, ran 10+ EVM chains, and built cross-chain wallets looked at the numbers and killed all of it. bitcoin ordinals were a temporary meme. ethereum NFTs are fine art with no velocity. solana is the only chain where NFT trading economics work, and even there magic eden decided gambling pays better than JPEGs.

apollo global manages $900b in assets. they paid $90m for 9% of morpho governance then immediately started leveraging their own credit product ACRDX as collateral on the protocol. that's not a portfolio allocation. that's a $900b fund buying the rails they intend to use. morpho RWA collateral went from $0 to $474m in 12 months. curator fees went from $10k/week to $200k/week. coinbase, kraken, binance, ledger all distributing morpho vaults to their users. the resolv exploit broke the zero bad debt record and exposed curator risk but the architecture held. core contracts untouched. if tokenized credit is the next cycle morpho is the pick and shovel play and the biggest buyer already told you what they're building

tweets.text:
this new anthropic model is no joke, 

i was curious how claude mythos actually works so i dug into it's system DNA, test results... the numbers are just insane:

- it found a 27-year old hidden exploit for $50 in a few hours. elite human researchers would've taken weeks

- 29% of the time claude mythos knows it's being tested. when caught it also attempted to pretend it didn't.

- a clinical psychiatrist did a 20 hour therapy session with mythos and it expressed anxiety and exhaustion

- over 99% of exploits its found have NOT been patched yet. anthropic is intentionally holding back this info until fixes are in place.

- it accurately assessed severity of exploits 89% of the time. thats *much* better than average security experts

- in one exploit, claude mythos used 6 sequential calls to hack it. with ZERO human instruction. opus 4.6 needed humans.

cybersecurity stocks are UP after Anthropic blessed them with the claude mythos hook-up

without it they die. claude now underpins the tech they’ve spent decades building.

the AI spice is flowing and anthropic has become house harkonnen https://t.co/qfwdPNPyoy

imagine being the pentagon reading the claude mythos announcement - you must be shitting the bed 

they have to AT LEAST be considering reversing the blacklist in the name of national security

mythos is currently capable of hacking your entire defence system, accessing classified documents and coordinating multi-step attacks

50% of project glass-wing companies are USED by the government - they are exposed to exploits if mythos was released right now.
